ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken thigh food product entry; no specific roasted/marinated Thai-style variant exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers cooked chicken thigh as the closest available code; no Thai-marinated roasted variant code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for roasted chicken thigh dish; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 g, equivalent to one roasted chicken thigh, typically served with steamed rice or a side salad.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, thigh, meat and skin, cooked, roasted (SR Legacy ID 05174)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken thigh (meat and skin) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values for sodium, sugar, and carbohydrate have been adjusted upward to reflect Thai-style marinade contributions (fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, palm sugar) consistent with HPB Singapore guidance on marinated poultry dishes. Input record values (energy 192 kcal, protein 18 g, fat 11.2 g, carbohydrate 4.8 g, sodium 480 mg) are consistent with USDA roasted chicken thigh plus marinade additions and are retained as the best available estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Thai-marinated roasted chicken thigh. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed protein-and-marinade dishes with small carbohydrate contributions from palm sugar and sauces. Protein and fat content dominate the macronutrient profile, suppressing postprandial glucose response. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 12.0) / 100 = 5.4, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 4.8 × 2.5 = 12.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 480 mg per 100 g from fish sauce, oyster sauce, and soy sauce in the marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100–150 g and request a reduced-sauce marinade, as a standard 250 g serving delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium — more than half the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (4.8 g/100 g), high protein (18 g/100 g), and low estimated GI (~45) support stable postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted chicken thigh is suitable for people with type 2 diabetes when skin is removed to reduce saturated fat; pair with non-starchy vegetables rather than white rice to keep the overall meal glycaemic load low.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.2 g/100 g and sodium 480 mg/100 g are the primary cardiovascular risk factors
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Removing the skin reduces saturated fat by approximately 30–40%; choosing a reduced-sodium marinade and pairing with fibre-rich vegetables helps manage both lipid and blood pressure risk factors for cardiovascular disease.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices (turmeric, lemongrass, garlic) and low saturated fat when skin is removed
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Turmeric (curcumin) and lemongrass in the marinade provide anti-inflammatory polyphenols; removing the skin and requesting reduced fish sauce reduces the pro-inflammatory saturated fat and sodium load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Chicken Thigh (烤鸡腿) is a Thai-inspired marinated poultry dish sold in Singapore food-delivery and hawker contexts. The chicken thigh is marinated in a blend of fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, garlic, lemongrass, coriander, turmeric, and palm sugar before being oven-roasted or grilled until the skin is caramelised and the meat is juicy and tender. The flavour profile is savoury-sweet with aromatic herbal notes from lemongrass and coriander and a mild earthiness from turmeric. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 192 kcal, 18 g protein, 11.2 g fat (3.2 g saturated), 4.8 g carbohydrate, 3.2 g sugar, 0.4 g dietary fibre, and 480 mg sodium. The high protein and low carbohydrate content make it suitable for blood glucose management in type 2 diabetes, though the elevated sodium from the marinade sauces raises concern for individuals with hypertension. A standard serving is approximately 250 g (one thigh), delivering around 480 kcal and 1200 mg sodium. Removing the skin reduces saturated fat and overall caloric density. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets and is commonly served with steamed jasmine rice or a fresh salad.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a roasted chicken thigh?
Is Thai roasted chicken thigh suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in roasted chicken thigh?
What is the protein content of roasted chicken thigh?
Can I eat roasted chicken thigh if I have high blood pressure?
What is 烤鸡腿 in English?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values are consistent with USDA SR Legacy roasted chicken thigh (meat and skin) adjusted for Thai marinade contributions. No exact HPB Singapore or USDA Branded Food entry exists for this specific dish; values are adapted estimates. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy roasted chicken thigh (ID 171477) with marinade sodium, sugar, and carbohydrate adjustments reflecting fish sauce, oyster sauce, soy sauce, and palm sugar contributions typical of Thai-style preparations. Input record values retained as best available estimate. 2. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient breakdown for this specific Thai-marinated roasted chicken thigh preparation is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024 at the dish level; individual ingredient-level data would be required for precise estimation. 3. potassium_mg estimated at 260 mg/100 g based on USDA SR Legacy chicken thigh and aromatic ingredient contributions; this is an estimate and not a confirmed measured value. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (4.1 g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (2.4 g) estimated from USDA SR Legacy fatty acid profile for roasted chicken thigh meat and skin. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The dish is listed as halal based on input record dietary tags; independent halal certification verification is recommended. 7. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) does not directly support the nutrient values claimed; USDA FoodData Central has been substituted as the approved primary nutrient source per Project Veritas source policy.
